prompt: Compare deribit options BTC and deribit options ETH: which has the richer 1-week implied volatility and steeper short-term skew according to Velo options rows?
expectedReason: homepage showcase categorical charts need multi-row comparisons
responsePreview:
Based on the Velo options rows for Deribit (last 24h, hourly resolution, fetched 2026-05-14T12:57:14.806Z):

| Metric | BTC | ETH |
|---|---|---|
| 1W IV — 24h mean | 0.35895 | 0.45201 |
| 1W IV — latest | 0.36653 | 0.45707 |
| 1W Skew — 24h mean | 0.09318 | 0.03255 |
| 1W Skew — latest | 0.12311 | 0.04715 |

**Takeaway**
- **Richer 1-week implied volatility:** **ETH** — it trades at a roughly 9.3 vol-point premium over BTC across the day (latest ~45.7% vs ~36.7%).
- **Steeper short-term skew:** **BTC** — its 1-week skew averages about 0.093 and has widened to ~0.123 most recently, versus ETH’s much flatter ~0.033 average and ~0.047 latest.

The accompanying chart shows ETH holding a consistent IV premium throughout the window, while BTC exhibits materially higher skew across all hourly prints.
